metadata.disclaimer: This JSON is for reference only. Policies change frequently. Always verify current requirements directly in Amazon Seller Central before listing. Sellers are responsible for compliance with all applicable federal and state laws.
metadata.policy_principle: Do not assume a product is permitted because it is not explicitly listed in prohibited examples, or because an existing ASIN exists. When in doubt, do not list.

restricted_categories.name: Alcohol
allowed: Alcoholic beverages sold by pre-approved selling partners
Non-alcoholic beverages with 0% alcohol-by-volume
Non-alcoholic beverages with greater than 0% and less than 0.5% alcohol-by-volume sold by pre-approved selling partners
Alcohol-related accessories and products, including corkscrews, decanters, and containers
Alcohol-related memorabilia and collectibles that do not contain alcohol
Distillation kits used for distilling oil, water, or other non-alcoholic beverages or products
prohibited: Alcoholic and non-alcoholic beverages over 0.0% alcohol-by-volume (except from pre-approved selling partners)
Alcohol distillation kits that reference alcohol making (e.g., beer, wine, and spirits)
Liquor licenses
Any product marketed for customers over 21 years of age
Any product that encapsulates raw alcohol

restricted_categories.name: Animals & Animal-Related Products
allowed: Live insects (e.g., bait worms, feeder insects)
Live shellfish and crustaceans intended for human consumption (e.g., live lobster, clams)
Faux/synthetic animal products (e.g., faux fur, faux feathers)
Pet food and treats
Pet supplies and accessories
Taxidermy of non-endangered, legally obtained animals with documentation
Mounted insects and butterfly displays (legally sourced)
prohibited: Live pets (dogs, cats, birds, hamsters, reptiles, fish, etc.)
Livestock and farm animals
Plant pests (e.g., invasive insects, nematodes not approved for agricultural use)
Endangered or threatened species or their parts (fur, feathers, ivory, bones, shells) regulated under CITES or ESA
Marine mammals or their parts (regulated under Marine Mammal Protection Act)
Products made from shark fins
Products made from cat or dog fur
Migratory bird feathers (regulated under Migratory Bird Treaty Act)
Illegal wildlife products without proper CITES permits
Bear bile products
Seal products (restricted under US Marine Mammal Protection Act)

restricted_categories.name: Art - Fine Art
allowed: One-of-a-kind original works by a named living artist (paintings, drawings, photographs)
Limited edition fine art prints with certificate of authenticity
Secondary-sale works (resale of authenticated art previously sold)
Photography prints by named artist
Two-dimensional artwork (paintings, prints, drawings)
prohibited: Three-dimensional artwork (sculptures) — not permitted in Amazon Fine Art Store
Archaeological or anthropological art
Mass-produced decorative art or posters
Promotional posters (e.g., movie, music, or brand posters)
Artwork without a named artist
Textile art
Unlicensed reproductions or copies of copyrighted artworks
Art listed without proper provenance or authenticity documentation

restricted_categories.name: Art - Home Decor
allowed: Mass-produced decorative wall art and prints
Home decor artwork with proper licensing
Decorative posters and canvas prints (non-promotional)
prohibited: Unlicensed use of copyrighted images or artwork
Products making false claims of being original fine art
Confederate flag imagery and similar offensive materials (see Offensive Materials category)
Promotional branded merchandise without authorization

restricted_categories.name: Automotive and Powersports
allowed: Auto parts and accessories with accurate fitment data
Tires complying with DOT safety standards
Powersports parts and accessories with correct FITMENT/YMME data
Automotive chemicals meeting safety and labeling requirements
OBD-II diagnostic tools for personal vehicle use
prohibited: Products that defeat emissions controls (e.g., catalytic converter deletes, EGR deletes, DPF deletes)
Engine defeat devices not meeting EPA Certificate of Conformity (COC) requirements
Street use of off-road-only exhaust systems or tuners that violate Clean Air Act
Airbag components not meeting FMVSS standards
Seatbelts and safety restraints not meeting federal standards
Products marketed to circumvent state vehicle inspection requirements
Odometer rollback devices
VIN alteration tools

restricted_categories.name: Composite Wood Products
allowed: Composite wood products that comply with TSCA Title VI (formaldehyde emission standards)
Products with CARB Phase 2 compliant certification
Solid wood products (not composite)
Products with third-party EPA-recognized TSCA Title VI TPC certification
prohibited: Composite wood products (hardwood plywood, medium-density fiberboard, particleboard) not meeting TSCA Title VI formaldehyde emission standards
Products containing regulated composite wood that does not meet EPA TSCA Title VI standards
Products without required TSCA Title VI compliance documentation or labeling

restricted_categories.name: Cosmetics and Skin and Hair Care
allowed: FDA-compliant cosmetics with complete ingredient lists (INCI format)
Shampoos, conditioners, lotions, and makeup meeting labeling requirements
Sunscreens meeting FDA OTC drug monograph requirements
Hair care products without prohibited ingredients
Properly labeled professional-use cosmetics
Minoxidil products with 5% or less concentration (OTC)
Hydrogen peroxide products up to 12% concentration for cosmetic use
prohibited: Contact lenses (require prescription and are classified as medical devices)
Products with more than 5% minoxidil
Products with more than 12% hydrogen peroxide
Body washes or scrubs with microbeads (polyethylene microplastics)
Products containing prohibited FDA ingredients (e.g., mercury, chloroform, bithionol)
Products making drug claims (e.g., 'cures acne', 'treats eczema') without FDA drug approval
Products claiming to be 'FDA approved' (FDA does not approve cosmetics)
Products with false or misleading labeling
Cosmetics tested on animals (if marketed as cruelty-free but are not)
Products not labeled in English
Prescription-only cosmetic drugs (e.g., tretinoin, prescription-strength retinoids)

restricted_categories.name: CPAP Cleaning and Disinfecting Devices
allowed: CPAP cleaning devices cleared by FDA as Class II medical devices
Manual CPAP cleaning supplies (brushes, soap, wipes) without pesticidal claims
CPAP mask wipes and cleaning solutions meeting FDA standards
prohibited: Ozone-based CPAP sanitizers not cleared by the FDA (FDA issued warning letters against these)
UV-light CPAP cleaners making unsubstantiated efficacy claims
Devices claiming to disinfect CPAP equipment without FDA 510(k) clearance
Products marketed to sanitize CPAP accessories using ozone without FDA authorization

restricted_categories.name: Currency, Coins, Cash Equivalents, and Gift Cards
allowed: Collectible coins in the Collectible Coins category (with prior Amazon approval)
Numismatic coins with proper grading and authentication
Coin collecting supplies and albums
Antique currency with clear collectible intent and proper labeling
prohibited: Paper money (US or foreign) as legal tender
Imitation or counterfeit currency
Commemorative medals marketed as currency
Altered legal tender (e.g., coins with holes, modified bills)
Gift cards (all brands, including Amazon gift cards)
Prepaid debit cards
Money orders or traveler's checks
Fake checks or novelty checks resembling real financial instruments
Counterfeit coins or paper money
Official government documents (passports, IDs, police badges, birth certificates) — real or fake

restricted_categories.name: Dietary Supplements
allowed: Supplements with FDA-compliant labeling (product name, quantity, Supplement Facts panel, manufacturer info in English)
Supplements in original, sealed, tamper-evident packaging
Supplements not containing FDA-prohibited ingredients
Products with Certificate of Analysis (CoA) from ISO/IEC 17025-accredited lab
Products with Letter of Guarantee from manufacturer
Vitamins, minerals, herbs, and amino acids meeting all labeling requirements
prohibited: Supplements containing FDA-prohibited or controlled substance ingredients
CBD (cannabidiol) products — classified as Schedule I controlled substance for supplement purposes
Products claiming to diagnose, cure, treat, or prevent any disease (drug claims without approval)
Products with 'FDA approved' labeling (FDA does not approve dietary supplements)
Supplements with ingredients not recognized as safe (GRAS) or not complying with New Dietary Ingredient (NDI) requirements
Products without complete Supplement Facts panel
Products not sealed in original manufacturer packaging
Weight loss supplements making unsubstantiated extreme claims
Supplements containing ephedra/ephedrine alkaloids
Products containing androstenedione or other anabolic steroid precursors
Products marketed as sexual enhancement drugs containing undisclosed pharmaceutical ingredients

restricted_categories.name: Drugs and Drug Paraphernalia
allowed: OTC medications meeting FDA requirements with proper labeling
Medically approved drug testing kits
Drug education materials and books
Legally sold herbal products not classified as controlled substances
prohibited: Prescription drugs of any kind
Controlled substances (Schedule I–V) including marijuana/cannabis, heroin, cocaine, methamphetamine
Vaccines, antibiotics, and prescription biologics
Drug paraphernalia designed for illicit drug use (pipes, bongs, syringes marketed for drug use)
Precursor chemicals used to manufacture controlled substances
Products marketed to pass drug tests (masking agents)
Synthetic cannabinoids (e.g., K2, Spice)
Bath salts or designer drugs with psychoactive properties
Nitrous oxide chargers marketed for recreational inhalation
Poppers (amyl/butyl nitrite) marketed as recreational inhalants
Any product marketed for illegal drug use

restricted_categories.name: Electronics
allowed: Electronics that are legal for sale in the US
Electronics complying with applicable industry standards (FCC, UL, CE)
Electronics that do not interfere with other electronic devices
Electronics that do not circumvent technical controls
Radio frequency devices with valid FCC ID
Lithium batteries meeting UN38.3 and DOT requirements with proper documentation
prohibited: Cell phone signal boosters/jammers (illegal under FCC regulations)
GPS jammers
Radar jammers or detectors (illegal in some states)
IMSI catchers (stingrays) marketed to general public
Devices that unlock cell phones in violation of DMCA
Streaming media players enabling unauthorized access to copyrighted content (preloaded Kodi boxes)
SIM card cloning devices
Electronic devices that emit RF radiation above FCC limits without FCC authorization
Products marketed to intercept communications without consent
Chip tuning/ECU flash tools marketed specifically to circumvent emissions controls
Radio frequency devices without FCC ID where required

restricted_categories.name: Explosives, Weapons, and Related Items
allowed: Non-lethal ammunition (rubber bullets, bean bag rounds) for law enforcement use
BB guns and air guns (with proper age and use disclaimers)
Knives and multi-tools used for hunting, fishing, or camping (non-prohibited types)
Crossbows and compound bows
Safety and trigger locks for firearms
Gun cleaning kits and legally sold accessories (non-conversion)
Muzzleloaders and black powder firearms (where federally legal)
Blank-firing replica firearms (clearly marked)
Firearm parts not regulated as firearms (e.g., stocks, grips, sights)
prohibited: Firearms (handguns, rifles, shotguns) — no exceptions
Fully automatic weapons or conversion kits (e.g., auto sears, switches)
Suppressors/silencers (require NFA registration, not sold on Amazon)
Bump stocks and binary triggers (federally regulated or banned)
High-capacity magazines where prohibited by state or federal law
Short-barreled rifles or shotguns without NFA documentation
Undetectable firearms (3D printed guns, ghost guns)
Explosive devices of any kind
Switchblades, ballistic knives, gravity knives (where federally prohibited)
Disguised weapons (belt buckle knives, cane swords, lipstick tasers)
Brass knuckles (illegal in many US states)
Tasers and stun guns marketed for offensive use
Incendiary rounds or tracer ammunition
Armor-piercing ammunition
Assault weapon accessories (e.g., forward pistol grips on pistols creating illegal NFA items)
Pellet guns marketed to look exactly like real firearms

restricted_categories.name: Export Controls
allowed: EAR99 items with no export control restrictions to permitted countries
Products with valid US export license where required
Publicly available technology and software with EAR license exception
prohibited: Items on the US Commerce Control List (CCL) sold without proper export license
Dual-use technology exported to sanctioned countries (OFAC list: Cuba, Iran, North Korea, Syria, Russia for controlled items)
Military items requiring State Department ITAR license
Encryption technology exported in violation of EAR
Nuclear, chemical, or biological materials subject to export controls
Night vision equipment exported without license
Products sold to denied parties on the BIS Denied Parties List

restricted_categories.name: Food & Beverage
allowed: FDA-registered food products with compliant labeling (Nutrition Facts, ingredient list, allergen declarations)
Foods meeting applicable safety standards (FSMA, HACCP)
Packaged foods with clearly marked expiration or best-by dates
USDA-inspected meat and poultry (refrigerated or frozen, from approved sellers)
Organic products with valid USDA organic certification
Food products not containing prohibited or controlled ingredients
prohibited: Betel nut products
Products containing horse meat
Kaffir lime leaves imported from Thailand (agricultural import restriction)
Raw or undercooked animal products not meeting USDA/FDA standards
Foie gras in states where prohibited (e.g., California)
Unpasteurized (raw) milk or dairy products for human consumption
Products containing controlled substances (e.g., khat, coca leaf products)
Absinthe with thujone above legal limits
Products with false or misleading health claims
Food products without required FDA labeling or allergen declarations
Shark fin products (prohibited in many US states)
Recalled food products
Food products past expiration date

restricted_categories.name: Gambling & Lottery
allowed: Non-functional slot machines for display or novelty purposes
Casino-themed toys and games (non-functional gambling devices)
Poker chips and playing cards for recreational use
Board games and card games with gambling themes
Books about gambling strategy
prohibited: Lottery tickets (state or otherwise)
Scratch-off lottery tickets
Coin-operated slot machines that pay out money or credits
Online gambling software or accounts
Sports betting accounts or credits
Any product that facilitates illegal gambling activity
Bingo machines used for cash-prize games
Raffle tickets

restricted_categories.name: Hazardous and Prohibited Items
allowed: Hazardous materials with proper safety data sheets (SDS) and FBA hazmat pre-approval
Consumer products with small quantities of regulated chemicals meeting CPSC requirements
Properly packaged and labeled household chemicals (cleaners, solvents) complying with OSHA HazCom
Lithium batteries meeting UN38.3 transport requirements with documentation
prohibited: Aerosols or compressed gas canisters not meeting DOT hazmat standards
Products containing asbestos
Products containing polychlorinated biphenyls (PCBs)
Products containing lead above legal limits in consumer goods
Radioactive materials for consumer use
Medical waste or sharps containers not properly sealed
Pyrotechnics and fireworks
Flammable solids or self-reactive substances not meeting packaging requirements
Products containing mercury (e.g., mercury thermometers, certain batteries) without proper labeling
Chlorofluorocarbon (CFC) products
Products containing banned pesticide substances (e.g., DDT, chlordane)

restricted_categories.name: Human Parts & Burial Artifacts
allowed: Anatomical models and replicas for medical/educational purposes (clearly synthetic)
Medical skeletons or replica bones made of plastic or resin
Hair extensions and wigs (commercially produced)
Donated blood and tissue for medical purposes through licensed institutions only
prohibited: Real human skulls, bones, or skeletal remains
Human organs, tissues, or blood for commercial sale
Native American burial artifacts and funerary objects (NAGPRA protected)
Items removed from burial sites in violation of applicable laws
Products made from real human remains
Hair from prisoners or exploitation victims
Human embryos or fetuses

restricted_categories.name: Jewelry & Precious Gems
allowed: Authentic jewelry with accurate metal type and purity labeling (e.g., 14K gold, 925 silver)
Gemstones with accurate quality disclosures (carat, cut, color, clarity for diamonds)
Lab-created gemstones clearly labeled as synthetic
Costume jewelry clearly labeled as non-precious
Watches with authentic brand authorization
prohibited: Counterfeit or fake designer jewelry (e.g., fake Cartier, Tiffany)
Jewelry with inaccurate metal content labeling (e.g., gold-plated sold as solid gold)
Diamonds or gemstones sold without proper grading disclosure
Blood diamonds or conflict diamonds (Kimberley Process non-compliance)
Jewelry made with ivory or products from endangered species
Lead-containing jewelry marketed for children (violates CPSIA)
Cadmium-containing jewelry above legal limits

restricted_categories.name: Laser Products
allowed: Laser pointers classified as Class I (safe under all conditions) or Class II (low power, visible)
Laser levels and measuring tools (Class I or II)
Laser printers and optical disc drives (built-in lasers, Class I)
FDA-cleared laser medical or cosmetic devices
Class IIIa/3R laser pointers under 5mW with proper safety labeling
prohibited: Children's toys with lasers exceeding hazard Class I
Class IIIB (3B) and Class IV (4) lasers unless authorized for sale by FDA Center for Devices and Radiological Health (CDRH)
Laser pointers above 5mW marketed for general use
Handheld lasers marketed to shine at aircraft (illegal under 18 U.S.C. § 39A)
High-power laser modules without CDRH variance or exemption
Laser products without required FDA radiation safety labeling

restricted_categories.name: Lighting
allowed: LED, CFL, and incandescent bulbs meeting FTC Lighting Facts label requirements
Luminaires (light fixtures) meeting UL or ETL safety certification standards
Specialty lighting (grow lights, stage lights) with proper labeling
Energy Star certified lighting products
Bulbs meeting applicable DOE energy efficiency standards
prohibited: General service incandescent lamps not meeting DOE energy efficiency standards (largely phased out)
Lighting products with false energy efficiency claims
Luminaires without required UL or equivalent safety certification
UV germicidal lamps making unsubstantiated pesticidal claims without EPA device compliance
Lighting products failing FCC Part 15 limits for electromagnetic interference

restricted_categories.name: Lock Picking & Theft Devices
allowed: Lock picks and locksmith tools marketed for legitimate locksmithing professionals
Bump keys marketed for locksport or professional locksmithing
Practice locks for lockpicking hobbyists (locksport)
Lock picking books and educational materials for legitimate use
prohibited: Devices specifically designed or marketed to steal vehicles (e.g., relay attack amplifiers for keyless entry theft)
Jamming devices that block car key fob signals to enable theft
Slim jims and car opening tools marketed for vehicle theft
RFID blocking bypass tools marketed for theft
Any lock bypass or picking tool marketed explicitly for illegal entry or theft
Master key systems for restricted systems (e.g., apartment complex master keys)

restricted_categories.name: Medical Devices and Accessories
allowed: FDA-cleared or FDA-approved Class I and Class II medical devices
OTC medical devices with proper labeling (e.g., blood pressure monitors, thermometers, bandages)
Prescription medical devices sold only to licensed medical professionals with proper documentation
Class I exempt devices (e.g., tongue depressors, bandages, manual stethoscopes)
Hearing aids (OTC hearing aids post-2022 FDA rule)
FDA-registered diabetic test strips that are not adulterated or misbranded
prohibited: Class III medical devices without FDA Premarket Approval (PMA)
Prescription-only devices sold to general consumers without authorization
Unapproved or counterfeit medical devices
Diabetic test strips obtained through fraudulent means (Medicare/Medicaid diversion)
Devices incorporating Class IIIB or IV lasers without FDA authorization
Devices making claims to diagnose or treat disease without FDA clearance
Non-FDA-cleared COVID-19 diagnostic tests
Devices banned by FDA (e.g., powdered surgeon's gloves, certain neurovascular catheters)

restricted_categories.name: Offensive and Controversial Materials
allowed: Historical books, documentaries, and educational materials about controversial topics
Satirical products with clear satirical intent
Products representing cultural heritage from the community they represent
prohibited: Products that promote, glorify, or celebrate hatred, violence, or discrimination based on race, religion, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, disability, or other protected characteristics
Nazi, white supremacist, or KKK memorabilia and paraphernalia
Confederate battle flag merchandise (removed from Amazon's catalog)
Products that glorify or celebrate mass shootings, genocides, or terrorist acts
Products depicting minors in a sexual manner
Products using slurs or hate speech as their primary design element
Blackface imagery or other historically offensive racial caricatures
Products mocking victims of atrocities or tragedies

restricted_categories.name: Other Restricted Products
allowed: Legally sold real estate listings through Amazon's approved channels
Legitimate subscriptions and memberships
Legal fireworks in states where permitted (seller must comply with state laws)
prohibited: Real estate (prohibited except through authorized channels)
Spy phones or tracking devices marketed for covert surveillance without consent
Fake or fraudulent reviews or review manipulation services
Products that infringe on intellectual property rights
Recalled products
Products marketed with false or unsubstantiated health claims
Items associated with human trafficking
Pyramid scheme or multi-level marketing enrollment kits
Works depicting child exploitation or non-consensual sexual acts

restricted_categories.name: Pest Control Products and Pesticides
allowed: EPA-registered pesticides with valid EPA Registration Number on label
Pesticide devices with valid EPA Establishment Number (e.g., bug zappers, ultrasonic repellers, UV traps)
Minimum risk pesticides exempt from registration under FIFRA 25(b) with compliant labeling
FIFRA-exempt pesticidal devices (e.g., mechanical traps, flyswatters)
Pesticides registered for the intended use and labeled correctly for US sale
prohibited: Unregistered pesticides not qualifying for an exemption
Pesticides with false or misleading efficacy claims
Pesticides not registered for their claimed use (e.g., a pesticide only registered for agricultural use sold for home use)
Cancelled or suspended EPA-registered pesticides
Pesticides in packaging that does not match the EPA-approved label
Products making pesticidal claims without EPA registration or FIFRA exemption
Pesticides banned by EPA (e.g., DDT, chlordane, aldrin)
Pesticide devices without EPA Establishment Number making efficacy claims beyond what data supports

restricted_categories.name: Plant and Seed Products
allowed: Non-invasive plant species legally sold domestically
Seeds for legal crops and flowers with proper labeling
USDA APHIS-approved imported plants with phytosanitary certificates
Noxious weed-free certified seeds
Tissue culture plants from approved domestic sources
prohibited: Cannabis seeds or cannabis plants (Schedule I controlled substance)
Plants or seeds on the Federal Noxious Weed list
Invasive plant species prohibited from sale or transport by USDA or state agencies
Imported plants without required USDA APHIS phytosanitary permits
Endangered plant species protected under CITES or ESA
Kaffir lime trees/plants imported from Thailand
Seeds marketed for drug production (e.g., poppies marketed for opium yield)

restricted_categories.name: Postage Meters and Stamps
allowed: Collectible/philatelic stamps clearly intended for collecting (not postage use)
Stamp collecting accessories (albums, mounts, hinges)
Canceled stamps (clearly used/cancelled)
prohibited: Unused postage stamps for mailing purposes (USPS has sole authority)
Postage meters or meter ink machines (only authorized USPS vendors may sell)
Counterfeit postage stamps
Prepaid postage labels not obtained through official channels
Products intended to defraud postal services

restricted_categories.name: Prohibited Product Claims
allowed: Structure/function claims for supplements with proper FDA disclaimer (e.g., 'supports immune health*' with disclaimer)
Accurate and truthful product descriptions with substantiated claims
Comparative claims with valid data and FTC-compliant substantiation
prohibited: Disease claims for supplements or cosmetics without FDA drug approval (e.g., 'cures cancer', 'treats diabetes')
Miracle cure claims without scientific substantiation
'FDA approved' claims for products FDA does not approve (supplements, cosmetics, food)
Guaranteed extreme weight loss or muscle gain claims without evidence
Unsubstantiated environmental claims (e.g., 'biodegradable', 'eco-friendly', 'carbon neutral') without certification
Claims that a product is effective against COVID-19 or other diseases without FDA authorization
'As seen on TV' claims that are false
Testimonial or endorsement claims that do not reflect typical results
Claims that imply a product has been endorsed or approved by a government agency when it has not

restricted_categories.name: Recalled Products
allowed: Products that have been cleared or remediated after a voluntary recall with documentation
Products that were subject to a recall but have been corrected and meet current safety standards
prohibited: Any product subject to a mandatory or voluntary recall by CPSC, FDA, NHTSA, or other agency
Products subject to a safety warning or stop-sale order
Recalled children's products of any kind
Recalled automotive parts (NHTSA recalls)
Recalled food, drug, or dietary supplement products
Recalled medical devices

restricted_categories.name: Recycling Electronics
allowed: Refurbished electronics that are fully functional and meet Amazon's renewed standards
Used electronics sold accurately as used
Electronics recycling bins/containers for consumer collection
prohibited: Non-functional electronics sold as functional
Electronics containing hazardous materials (CRTs with lead, PCBs) sold for consumer use without proper handling disclosure
E-waste sold for resale without disclosure of non-functionality
Electronics in violation of state e-waste disposal laws (e.g., some states require manufacturer take-back)

restricted_categories.name: Refrigerants: Ozone-Depleting Substances and Substitutes
allowed: EPA SNAP-approved refrigerants sold to certified technicians (Section 608 certified)
Refrigerants in containers under 2 lbs for self-contained small appliances (EPA exemption)
HFO refrigerants and other non-ozone-depleting substitutes approved under EPA SNAP program
prohibited: CFC refrigerants (e.g., R-11, R-12) — banned under Clean Air Act
HCFC refrigerants (e.g., R-22) sold to non-certified purchasers after phasedown
Refrigerants in containers over 2 lbs sold to non-EPA Section 608 certified technicians
Refrigerants vented intentionally into the atmosphere
Counterfeit or non-SNAP-approved refrigerant substitutes
Refrigerants sold in violation of EPA Clean Air Act Section 608 requirements

restricted_categories.name: Strike Anywhere Matches
allowed: Safety matches (strike-on-box only)
Windproof lighters and survival fire starters
Waterproof matches in survival kits (safety type)
prohibited: Strike anywhere matches (prohibited from sale on Amazon due to DOT hazmat shipping restrictions — classified as a flammable solid)
Matches in bulk quantities violating DOT hazmat rules

restricted_categories.name: Surveillance Equipment
allowed: Home security cameras with clear disclosure in product listings
Baby monitors
Nanny cameras sold with full disclosure of the recording capability
Network monitoring tools for IT/cybersecurity professionals
GPS trackers marketed for asset tracking with owner consent
Dash cameras for vehicles
prohibited: Covert surveillance devices marketed explicitly for spying without consent (e.g., hidden cameras in smoke detectors, clocks, pens marketed for clandestine recording)
GPS trackers marketed to track individuals without their knowledge or consent
Wiretapping or phone interception devices
Software marketed for covert phone monitoring (stalkerware)
Spy equipment marketed for corporate espionage
Devices marketed to intercept communications in violation of the Electronic Communications Privacy Act (ECPA)

restricted_categories.name: Tobacco & Tobacco-Related Products
allowed: Cigarette cases and holders
Cigar cutters and holders
Lighters (non-novelty, complying with CPSC lighter safety standard 16 CFR Part 1212)
Tobacco humidors
Ashtrays
Pipes and pipe cleaning tools
Cigarette rolling machines and papers (for legal tobacco use)
Beer and wine making kits (analogous leniency)
prohibited: Cigarettes, cigars, pipe tobacco, smokeless tobacco, and chewing tobacco
Electronic cigarettes (e-cigarettes) and vaping devices
Vape juice/e-liquid (nicotine or otherwise)
Nicotine pouches and oral nicotine products
Hookah tobacco (shisha)
Herbal cigarettes or bidis
Smokeless tobacco (snus, dip, chew)
Any tobacco product that has not received FDA PMTA (Premarket Tobacco Application) authorization
Flavored e-cigarettes or vaping products (banned by FDA for most flavors)
Synthetic nicotine products without FDA authorization
Tobacco products marketed to minors

restricted_categories.name: Warranties, Service Plans, Contracts, and Guarantees
allowed: Extended warranty plans offered by Amazon's own warranty partners (Amazon-authorized)
Manufacturer warranties included with physical products
Legitimate third-party protection plans from Amazon-approved providers
prohibited: Third-party warranties, service plans, or protection plans not approved by Amazon
Extended warranties sold as standalone products by unauthorized third parties
Service contracts or maintenance agreements sold independently
Guarantee products that misrepresent coverage or are not backed by a legitimate insurer

restricted_categories.name: Upholstered Furniture, Bedding, & Other Quilted Products
allowed: Upholstered furniture with required law label (Textile Fiber Products Identification Act)
Bedding and mattresses with proper fill content labeling
Products meeting applicable state flammability standards (e.g., California TB 117-2013)
Quilts, comforters, and pillows with accurate fill content labeling
prohibited: Upholstered furniture without required law labels (illegal under TFPIA)
Products using prohibited fill materials
Mattresses not meeting applicable flammability standards (16 CFR Part 1633)
Children's sleepwear not meeting flammability requirements (16 CFR Parts 1615/1616)
Products with mislabeled fill content (e.g., labeled as down but containing other fill)

restricted_categories.name: Federal Emissions – Products Requiring EPA Certificate of Conformity
allowed: New engines, vehicles, and equipment with valid EPA Certificate of Conformity (COC)
CARB-compliant products sold in California with Executive Order number
Off-road-only products clearly labeled and sold for off-road use only
EPA-certified small engines (lawn mowers, generators, chainsaws) with EPA emission label
prohibited: Fuel-powered engines or vehicles without EPA COC (violates Clean Air Act)
Engines or vehicles sold for street use that only have off-road COC
Emission defeat devices (tuners, ECU flashes) marketed for street use
Catalytic converter removal kits for road-use vehicles
EGR/DPF delete kits marketed for on-road vehicles
Non-compliant small off-road engines (SORE) or recreational vehicles
Marine engines without EPA COC

restricted_categories.name: Children's Footwear
allowed: Children's footwear meeting CPSIA requirements
Footwear tested by CPSC-accepted third-party laboratory with Children's Product Certificate (CPC)
Footwear without lead in surface coatings above 90ppm or total lead above 100ppm
Footwear with accurate size labeling and country of origin
prohibited: Children's footwear with lead content above CPSIA limits (>90ppm surface coating, >100ppm total)
Children's footwear with phthalate content above CPSIA limits
Footwear without required CPC (Children's Product Certificate)
Children's footwear without third-party CPSC-accepted lab testing
Footwear with small parts posing choking hazard not labeled appropriately
Drawstring-equipped children's footwear not meeting ASTM F1816 guidelines

restricted_categories.name: Children's Apparel
allowed: Children's clothing meeting CPSIA, CPSC, and FTC labeling requirements
Children's sleepwear meeting CPSC flammability standards (16 CFR Parts 1615/1616)
Children's apparel with Children's Product Certificate (CPC) from CPSC-accepted lab
Clothing with accurate fiber content and care labeling (FTC Textile Rules)
prohibited: Children's apparel with lead or phthalates above CPSIA limits
Children's sleepwear not meeting 16 CFR Parts 1615/1616 flammability requirements
Drawstring-equipped children's upper outerwear violating ASTM F1816
Children's apparel without required CPC
Clothing with inaccurate fiber content labeling (FTC violation)
Children's clothing with small parts or decorative elements posing choking hazard

restricted_categories.name: Automotive Tires, Wheels, and Rims
allowed: DOT-compliant tires with proper DOT serial number and UTQG rating
OEM-spec replacement wheels and rims
Off-road or track-use tires clearly marked as not for street use
New tires manufactured within the last 6 years (many states/Amazon require this)
Winter and all-season tires meeting applicable DOT standards
prohibited: Tires without DOT certification
Tires marketed for street use that are not DOT-rated
Tires that are structurally unsafe or past recommended service life
Counterfeit tires or tires with falsified DOT numbers
Used tires (prohibited in Amazon's catalog for safety reasons)
Tires with tire age over acceptable limits without disclosure
Wheels that do not meet SAE or industry load rating standards

restricted_categories.name: Washington State Expanded Polystyrene
allowed: Expanded polystyrene (EPS/Styrofoam) products sold outside Washington State
Non-EPS or recyclable alternative foam packaging products sold in Washington State
EPS products that qualify for specific exemptions under Washington State law (RCW 70A.530)
prohibited: Expanded polystyrene (EPS) food service products sold to Washington State customers (banned effective January 1, 2023 under RCW 70A.530)
Polystyrene foam cups, plates, bowls, and containers sold for food service use in Washington State
EPS loose-fill packaging peanuts sold for commercial use in Washington State
